verses.commentary: Wept over him. Kissed him. Immediate grief. Physical mourning. Love expressed. Final farewell.
Physicians embalmed. Egyptian custom. Medical procedure. Takes time.
Extended mourning. Near royal honors.
Tell him. Speak to Pharaoh. Tell him. Formal request.
Then I'll return. Oath mentioned (47:29-31). Tomb in Canaan. Request to go. Promise to return.
Permission granted. Honors the oath.
All Pharaoh's officials accompanied him, the dignitaries of his court and all the dignitaries of Egypt. State funeral.
Joseph's household. Brothers. Father's household.
International significance.
Near Jordan. At Jordan's edge. Loud mourning. Seven days. Traditional Jewish mourning (shiva).
Place called Abel Mizraim (אָבֵל מִצְרַיִם), mourning of Egypt. Observers impressed.
Obedience. Promise kept.
Carried to Canaan. Near Mamre. Cave of Machpelah. Family tomb. Purchased land.
Return as promised. Back to Egypt. Life continues.
Will Joseph take revenge? Guilt remains.
They send message.

Now please forgive the sins of the servants of the God of your father. Say to Joseph: Forgive brothers' sins and wrongs. Treating you badly. Please forgive servants of God of your father. Joseph wept. Appeal to forgive. Acknowledge sins. Badly treated. Invoke God of your father. Joseph weeps.
Dreams fulfilled again (37:7,9).
Joseph refuses to play God. Don't be afraid. Am I in the place of God? Only God judges and determines destinies. Joseph maintains humility even in power.

You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives. You intended harm. God intended good. Saving many lives. Classic verse. Human evil. Divine purpose. You meant evil. God meant good. Result: many saved. Providence and sovereignty. God uses evil for good.
Don't be afraid. Reassured them. Spoke kindly. Reassurance. Kind words. Grace extended.
All father's family. Permanent residence. Family together. 110 years. Full life. Blessed.
Extended blessing. Children on knees. Adoption gesture. Recognized as his.

God will come to your aid. To land promised to Abraham, Isaac, Jacob. Death approaching. Final words. Prophecy of Exodus. God will visit. Deliverance coming. Return to promised land. Covenant promises certain.
God will come to your aid. Oath required. Certain: God will come. Faith in promises. Burial in promised land.
After embalming. Death recorded. Egyptian burial. Coffin in Egypt. Far from promised land. Exodus will come. But promise remains.
